_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-5 ALS/LALS Intercept and Transfer of Care (Revised 6/5/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: ALS/LALS Intercept and Transfer of Care
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-5: ALS/LALS Intercept and Transfer of Care — MDHHS Approved 6/5/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern ALS or LALS (Limited Advanced Life Support — Specialist/AEMT) intercept with BLS units and formal transfer of care between EMS providers. MCAs must define intercept criteria and transfer of care processes. Proper transfer of care documentation protects patient safety and provider accountability.

sections.title: ALS/LALS Intercept Authorization
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has defined intercept criteria and process
ALS or LALS intercept may be requested by a BLS unit when ALS/LALS care is needed and the patient cannot wait for a primary ALS response
Intercept requests should be coordinated through dispatch or direct radio contact between crews
Intercept is appropriate when: patient condition requires ALS/LALS intervention AND transport time or scene time would benefit from intercept

sections.title: Transfer of Care — Process
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-5: Intercept — Transfer of Care
points: Formal transfer of care: receiving provider must receive a verbal report including patient name, age, chief complaint, assessment findings, vital signs, interventions performed, and response to treatment
The provider assuming care accepts responsibility for the patient from the moment of formal handoff
Verbal report must be acknowledged by the receiving provider before the transferring provider is released
Transfer of care is NOT complete until the receiving provider confirms receipt of report
Scene transfer of care: BLS crew may continue transport with ALS crew, or ALS crew may assume care with patient transfer to ALS unit

sections.title: Documentation
points: Both the transferring and receiving units must document the transfer of care in their respective PCRs
Transferring unit documents: time of transfer, receiving unit or provider, clinical status at time of transfer
Receiving unit documents: time care assumed, clinical status received, all interventions from point of assumption of care
Document intercept location and any scene time associated with intercept

sections.title: Responsibilities After Transfer
points: After formal transfer of care: receiving provider operates under their scope of practice and MCA protocols
Transferring crew is released from patient care responsibility after formal handoff
If ALS provider intercepts BLS during transport, coordinate handoff to minimize transport delay for time-sensitive patients

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize ALS/LALS intercept and define criteria and process
options: Authorized — MCA intercept criteria and process defined

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may separately authorize LALS (Specialist/AEMT level) intercept

deterioration_flags: Patient deteriorates before ALS intercept arrives — initiate BLS protocols appropriate to condition; do NOT delay BLS care awaiting intercept
Intercept crew unable to reach intercept point — redirect to hospital if transport time to hospital < intercept wait time
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — MCA must define intercept criteria and transfer of care process
Intercept: coordinate through dispatch or direct radio contact
Formal transfer of care: verbal report with name, age, complaint, assessment, vitals, interventions, response
Transfer is not complete until receiving provider confirms receipt
Both units document transfer in respective PCRs
LALS (Specialist) intercept requires separate MCA authorization
